title: Trading Spouses

jokes.text: So, it's like O'Doul's, but for weed.
jokes.setup: Introduction of O'Dweeds as marijuana with no THC
jokes.punchline: Direct comparison to non-alcoholic beer brand
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy of words with a clever product comparison that instantly communicates the absurd concept. Strong structure and memorable.

jokes.text: Shotgun, little buddy!
jokes.setup: Father sharing fake weed with baby
jokes.punchline: Treating infant like smoking buddy
jokes.explanation: Subversive parenting moment that's both shocking and character-specific to this oblivious dad.

jokes.text: This reminds me of when I was a girl and I used to smoke grass with all the negro jazz musicians.
jokes.setup: Elderly white woman reminiscing about fake weed
jokes.punchline: Casually racist terminology while describing her wild past
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work revealing casual racism mixed with unexpected past wildness. The discomfort is the comedy.

jokes.text: Come on, you old bitch.
jokes.setup: Man trying to get Martha to share fake weed
jokes.punchline: Sudden aggressive insult over something harmless
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of tension over something meaningless, showing how seriously they take fake weed.

jokes.text: Tastes like the chronic, and yet it's not.
jokes.setup: Jamaican accent discussing fake marijuana
jokes.punchline: Philosophical observation about tasteless fake weed
jokes.explanation: The Jamaican character's earnest delivery of this contradiction works well.

jokes.text: Wrong bag, man.
jokes.setup: Man praising fake weed that tastes like real weed
jokes.punchline: Reveals he accidentally gave him actual marijuana
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with maximum economy. Two words completely subvert the entire commercial premise.

jokes.text: From the makers of the 'Crack Patch'.
jokes.setup: Fake marijuana commercial ending
jokes.punchline: Company also makes nicotine patch parody for crack addiction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that reveals this company's entire absurd product line. Dark and memorable.

jokes.text: Dave's intro about And 1 mix tapes
jokes.setup: Setting up basketball mix tape segment
jokes.punchline: Genuine enthusiasm for street basketball culture
jokes.explanation: Setup material for the parody to follow, not really a joke itself.

jokes.text: No, he didn't, no, he didn't!
jokes.setup: Watching And 1 basketball footage
jokes.punchline: Dave's over-the-top reaction to mundane golf shot
jokes.explanation: Sets up the absurd juxtaposition of basketball commentary over other sports.

jokes.text: That's the baddest motherfucker I ever seen in my life!
jokes.setup: Continued basketball commentary over non-basketball footage
jokes.punchline: Extreme praise for presumably mundane action in other sport
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the absurd commentary mismatch. Dave's enthusiasm sells it.

jokes.text: Kobe... you're under arrest.
jokes.setup: Explanation of shouting player names during real-life activities
jokes.punchline: References Kobe Bryant's legal troubles
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly constructed setup-punchline with perfect timing and cultural relevance. Maximum impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: Rasheed Wallace.
jokes.setup: Continuation of player name concept
jokes.punchline: References Wallace's technical foul reputation during argument
jokes.explanation: Strong callback that builds on established premise. Wallace's reputation makes this land perfectly.

jokes.text: Damon Stoudamire.
jokes.setup: Continuation of player name concept
jokes.punchline: References Stoudamire's drug issues during workplace scenario
jokes.explanation: Another solid callback, though less universally known than previous references.

jokes.text: Sprewell...
jokes.setup: Boss asking for more effort, continuation of player concept
jokes.punchline: References Sprewell choking his coach during workplace violence
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant callback that perfectly matches the player's notorious incident to the workplace scenario.

jokes.text: Paul Pierce... Oh, ah.
jokes.setup: Continuation of player concept
jokes.punchline: References Pierce being stabbed, with pain sounds
jokes.explanation: Good callback though the reference is more obscure than others in the series.

jokes.text: Every pro athlete ever.
jokes.setup: Child support/paternity scenario
jokes.punchline: Suggests all pro athletes have this issue
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation that indicts entire profession while maintaining the format.

jokes.text: Wilt Chamberlain.
jokes.setup: Sexual scenario, continuation of player concept
jokes.punchline: References Chamberlain's famous claim of 20,000 sexual partners
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that uses Wilt's legendary sexual boasts. Maximum economy and impact.

jokes.text: Dennis Rodman. Oh, hell, no.
jokes.setup: Cross-dressing scenario, continuation of player concept
jokes.punchline: References Rodman's cross-dressing with horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Good callback with strong character reaction. Dave's horror sells the visual.

jokes.text: OJ...
jokes.setup: Murder scenario, continuation of player concept
jokes.punchline: References O.J. Simpson murder trial
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of the bit. Maximum taboo with perfect economy. This is legendary material.

jokes.text: Long fart sequence with diarrhea song
jokes.setup: Response to People magazine calling show immature
jokes.punchline: Extended toilet humor as deliberate immaturity
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about immaturity but relies heavily on juvenile humor. The self-awareness saves it from being completely cheap.

jokes.text: Mr. Deez? Deez nuts! I-I don't understand.
jokes.setup: White father asking to be called by first name
jokes.punchline: Classic 'deez nuts' joke followed by confused white reaction
jokes.explanation: Classic joke executed well with perfect character reactions. Todd's confusion sells the cultural divide.

jokes.text: Hell no. Only your mama calls me daddy.
jokes.setup: White boy asking to call Leonard daddy
jokes.punchline: Leonard's sexually charged refusal
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment that establishes Leonard's dominant personality through inappropriate humor.

jokes.text: Here, mop top, take my bag up to my room. And if I find out you been going through my shit, I'm gonna beat you in your ass, you understand?
jokes.setup: Leonard meeting white family
jokes.punchline: Immediately threatening the child with violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work showing cultural clash through Leonard's aggressive parenting style.

jokes.text: I don't smell no dinner cooking. Run on, make some grits.
jokes.setup: Leonard entering white household
jokes.punchline: Immediately ordering wife to cook stereotypical food
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy that plays with stereotypes while showing Leonard's domineering personality.

jokes.text: What the fuck is a parsnip?
jokes.setup: Todd explaining white family dinner foods
jokes.punchline: Leonard's profane confusion at unfamiliar vegetable
jokes.explanation: Perfect cultural divide moment. Leonard's genuine confusion and profanity land well.

jokes.text: Well, just so you know, his ass will be back april 13th.
jokes.setup: Sharron explaining Todd usually does dishes
jokes.punchline: Leonard's matter-of-fact refusal with specific date
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery that shows Leonard's complete unwillingness to adapt. The specific date makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You better check your tone, girl, get your inside voice on before I put your ass outside, mm.
jokes.setup: Sharron being sarcastic about lighting cigarette
jokes.punchline: Leonard's creative threat using 'inside voice' literally
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that turns parenting phrase into threat. Shows Leonard's dominance through creative language.

jokes.text: Well, go on upstairs and wash your ass and then we'll watch Martin Lawrence.
jokes.setup: Normal family evening routine
jokes.punchline: Leonard's crude language and cultural preferences
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Leonard's complete culture clash

jokes.text: Who the fuck is Renee 'Zellwedger'?
jokes.setup: Leonard looking at white family magazines
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces Renée Zellweger's name with confusion
jokes.explanation: Good cultural divide humor. The mispronunciation and profanity show Leonard's disconnect from white pop culture.

jokes.text: Time-out is sweet! Halle Berry, here I come.
jokes.setup: T-Mart being put in timeout as punishment
jokes.punchline: Celebrates timeout as opportunity for privacy/masturbation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of typical punishment. Shows how ineffective white parenting is on street-smart kid.

jokes.text: You're going on time-out. For 15 minutes, okay? Yeah... Starting now, T-Mart. Time-out!
jokes.setup: White father disciplining black child
jokes.punchline: Ineffective white parenting vs. strong-willed black child
jokes.explanation: Excellent parenting culture clash - shows different disciplinary approaches

jokes.text: G-g-g-g-g-g... G-Unit.
jokes.setup: White kid trying to be street after hanging with T-Mart
jokes.punchline: Awkward stuttering attempt at 50 Cent catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing white kid's awkward adoption of hip-hop culture.

jokes.text: that Leonard Washington is glad he made it out, go on.
jokes.setup: Leonard dropping white kid in the hood
jokes.punchline: Refers to himself in third person like he escaped the ghetto
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Leonard's superiority complex. Third-person reference adds to his grandiosity.

jokes.text: And if you need money, sell rocks, I heard that's what they do around here.
jokes.setup: Leonard giving advice to white kid in the hood
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests drug dealing as normal neighborhood activity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor showing Leonard's casual attitude toward serious crime. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Damn, bitch, what's this, a light saber or something?
jokes.setup: Leonard discovering vibrator in bedroom
jokes.punchline: Compares sex toy to Star Wars weapon
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with unexpected pop culture reference. Leonard's confusion is genuine and funny.

jokes.text: Actually, I'd feel more comfortable if I just pulled my penis through this hole.
jokes.setup: Sharron asking Todd to remove pajama bottoms for sex
jokes.punchline: Todd's awkward preference for pajama sex through opening
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Todd's sexual awkwardness and repression. The specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, baby, I don't go South of the border. That's just one thing that Leonard Washington don't do.
jokes.setup: Sexual encounter with Sharron
jokes.punchline: Leonard refuses oral sex using geographical metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor. The geographical metaphor and third-person reference are perfectly Leonard.

jokes.text: Now, I done heard of trimming the hedges, but you done scorched the earth.
jokes.setup: Sharron mentions being waxed
jokes.punchline: Leonard's elaborate metaphor for complete hair removal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating metaphor that's both original and perfectly delivered. One of the episode's best lines.

jokes.text: I smell your light saber.
jokes.setup: Awkward post-sex moment
jokes.punchline: References earlier vibrator discovery
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier moment. Leonard's direct observation style works well here.

jokes.text: Bitch, I'll never forgive you for this.
jokes.setup: Couples therapy session
jokes.punchline: Leonard's hostile response to being in therapy
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Leonard's complete resistance to therapy culture.

jokes.text: All right, I'ma tell you right now, I ain't crazy, I don't need no psychiatrist. And if you ever tell anybody I've been in here, I'll fuckin' kill ya.
jokes.setup: Therapist encouraging Leonard to open up
jokes.punchline: Leonard claims he's not crazy while making death threats
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure. Leonard's claim of sanity while threatening murder is beautifully contradictory.

jokes.text: Well then, confidentially, I am crazy, and I'll fuckin' kill ya.
jokes.setup: Therapist assuring confidentiality
jokes.punchline: Leonard admits he's crazy and repeats threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that flips his previous statement. The logic is absurdly perfect while maintaining the threat.

jokes.text: And for the first time in my life... I tasted brown sugar, and not in my oatmeal.
jokes.setup: Todd reflecting on his experience with black family
jokes.punchline: Sexual metaphor about interracial experience
jokes.explanation: Well-structured double entendre that fits Todd's awkward character perfectly.

jokes.text: Speaking of which, I learned that white people don't use washcloths. Did you know that?
jokes.setup: Leonard sharing what he learned about white people
jokes.punchline: Focuses on hygiene differences rather than deeper cultural insights
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor that plays on real cultural differences. Leonard's focus on hygiene over emotions is perfect.

jokes.text: Every time I used it, somebody else's pubic hair was in it. So use the cloth. Why your ass got to put the raw bar of soap in your butt and all this?
jokes.setup: Leonard explaining white hygiene problems
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of soap contamination and anal washing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that gets increasingly graphic. Leonard's disgust is genuine and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Although the penis-through-the-hole thing was kind of weird. And I am missing some of my draw's.
jokes.setup: Sharron comparing Todd and Leonard
jokes.punchline: References Todd's sexual quirk and implies Leonard stole underwear
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that adds new information about Leonard's kleptomania. Works as character detail.

jokes.text: Titty residue...
jokes.setup: Todd discovering something on phone
jokes.punchline: Identifies breast-related contamination with scientific terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Todd's clinical approach to sexuality. The scientific term for something crude is very Todd.

jokes.text: How old are you? I'm 35. I'm 27. Damn!
jokes.setup: Age discussion between Dave and woman
jokes.punchline: Dave's shock at the age difference
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction humor, functional but unremarkable

tmdb_overview: Dave delves into what would happen if couples attempted "Trading Spouses" and introduces a new family-oriented form of marijuana which contains no THC. Also featuring musical guest Blackstar.
